reviewer: new-demo build wave (in-browser runnability proof first, then headless-Chrome real download + real inference on the index page via CDP driver, mobile+desktop overflow + console/network checks, standalone visual review of the composited inpaint result)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference proven BEFORE building: an ORT-web@1.21.0 WASM harness (headless Chrome, no GPU) created a session from lama_fp32.onnx — inputs 'image' [1,3,512,512] f32 [0,1] + 'mask' [1,1,512,512] f32 {0,1}, output 'output' [1,3,512,512] f32 in [0,255]. On a structured test image with a central 128×128 hole (mask=1), the filled region came back with real structure (luminance variance 3728 in 0–255 space ⇒ std ≈ 61, not flat/blank/noise), confirming mask convention 1=hole and that LaMa synthesises content. transformers.js has no `lama`/Fourier-conv class and no inpainting pipeline task, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a module Worker. Headless index build-verify (CDP driver, fresh profile → Download ~208 MB → ready → rectangle-preset → Inpaint): real output, inference 12846 ms + composite 19 ms on WASM, mask area 15.9%, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Standalone visual proof reviewed: a masked archway of a cobbled alley reconstructed with continued street + wall texture (filled-centre luma std 0.086 = real structure).
rubric.notes: LaMa (WACV 2022, Apache-2.0) is a large-mask inpainting network; its Fourier convolutions let it continue periodic texture across big holes rather than smudge/clone-stamp.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed: output tensor dims [1,3,512,512] read back at runtime in the [0,255] range (measured min ≈ 22, max ≈ 233). We clamp to RGBA and COMPOSITE only the masked region back over the original pixels (final = orig·(1−mask) + lama·mask), so untouched areas are pixel-exact and there is no global colour shift or seam. Semantic check: the filled region is coherent background continuous with its surroundings (visual review of the composited alley), and the 'See inside' fill map shows exactly the painted region (indigo) over a dimmed original — proving the change is confined to the mask.
rubric.notes: image-to-image task template (dense per-pixel output grounded in the input + a mask) fits: the masked region is rewritten with plausible background, everything else copied through.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance names the exact model (Carve/LaMa-ONNX, linked), task (image-to-image / inpainting), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), both input shapes ('image' [1,3,512,512] / 'mask' [1,1,512,512], with 1=fill/0=keep stated), output [1,3,512,512] in [0,255], dtype (fp32, plain WASM EP, no WebGPU), size (~208 MB) and licence (Apache-2.0), and honestly notes transformers.js has no LaMa class. inference-off-main-thread = pass (new Worker + worker.js). No invented API.
rubric.notes: Fixed 512×512 network input is stated up front; arbitrary images and the user's mask are resized to 512 and the composited result resized back to the display aspect.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads through createModelLoader. The worker fetches the ONNX THROUGH Cache Storage (lama-inpaint-onnx-cache) with a content-length Response so lib/model-cache.js scans '/Carve/LaMa-ONNX/' and the shared loader auto-inits on a returning visit (no Download button); the per-model clear-cache control works. First-visit driver run showed the honest Download (~208 MB) state with streaming progress, then ready — never a silent large re-download.
rubric.notes: The 4 ladder pages share the same modelId → shared cache → auto-init; the multi-model page loads a SECOND model (Real-ESRGAN) through its own loader.

rubric.evidence: loads-clean = pass (0 console errors on load). Download progress streams from the worker into the loader progress bar; failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. An empty mask is caught with a readable .status.err ('paint a region to fill first'), never a silent no-op. Status uses role=status aria-live=polite. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) — runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner. A 512×512 pass is ~13 s on single-threaded WASM; the run status says so and the button is disabled with a working status during inference.
rubric.notes: Mask editing (brush/rectangle/clear) is cheap main-thread canvas work; the expensive ONNX pass runs only on Inpaint, off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised and labelled + keyboard-operable: the rights-safe example gallery, the drop-zone + file input, the Inpaint / Download-result buttons, the brush-size <input type=range>, the Brush/Erase-mask segmented toggle (aria-pressed), the Rectangle-preset and Clear-mask buttons, and the Pointer-Events mask overlay (role=application, tabindex=0, aria-label describing the brush + that the rectangle/clear buttons are below). The mask editor uses Pointer Events + setPointerCapture (unified mouse/touch/pen) with touch-action:none so a drag paints rather than scrolls; co-ordinates are converted from client space to canvas pixels so it works at any responsive size/DPR.
rubric.notes: Honest by design — the visitor marks the exact region; the rectangle preset gives a keyboard-only path to a valid mask without needing to draw.

rubric.evidence: The filled image, the fill map, and the mask-area/latency numbers all come from the on-device ORT-web run — no canned values. The output changes with the image, the painted mask, and the mask size (visible in the result, the fill map, and the wild page's growing-hole log), proving it tracks the real model, not a placeholder. Pixels outside the mask are the visitor's own image, composited through unchanged.
rubric.notes: The copy states plainly that LaMa reconstructs plausible content, not the truth — an eraser-and-filler, not an undelete; the wild page shows large holes going soft/generic in the centre.

rubric.evidence: images-have-alt = pass, buttons-have-names = pass, live-status-region = pass. The base/result/fill canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-label (image to edit / inpainted result / region the model filled); the paint overlay is role=application + tabindex=0 with an aria-label explaining the interaction and pointing to the rectangle/clear alternatives; the Brush/Erase toggle uses aria-pressed; the brush range and the mask-tool buttons are labelled; status is role=status aria-live=polite. :focus-visible styled on the overlay, toggle, drop-zone, and range.
rubric.notes: Because a freehand brush is inherently pointer-driven, the Rectangle-preset + Clear-mask buttons provide a fully keyboard-operable route to a usable mask — the accessible fallback for the drawing surface (see guidanceConsulted).

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop = pass, responsive-mobile = pass. CDP driver measured horizontal overflow = 0 px at BOTH desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 on the index page, AND a load-only overflow sweep of all 4 use-case pages measured 0 px at both viewports on each. Layout uses flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0 two-column (image | controls) that stacks on mobile; the gallery is an auto-fill minmax grid; the paint-stage is max-inline-size:640px with canvases at inline-size:100% block-size:auto; the mask overlay uses touch-action:none; tool buttons meet ~44px min-block-size. Light + dark inherit design-system variables.
rubric.notes: The mask overlay's client→canvas co-ordinate mapping via getBoundingClientRect keeps brushing accurate after the canvas is responsively scaled down on mobile.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense output composite (clamp tensor → RGBA, the orig·(1−mask)+lama·mask blend, the fill map, and the resize to display aspect — the systemic hotspot invariant 15 warns about) all run in the module Worker; the composited result and fill map are built with OffscreenCanvas and returned as transferred ImageBitmaps (zero-copy). The main thread only draws bitmaps and handles the lightweight brush strokes. Measured composite 19 ms in the worker. The ~13 s inference itself is entirely off the main thread, so it never blocks INP — the UI stays responsive and the button/status reflect the busy state honestly.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes — the heavy per-pixel work and the long ONNX pass are off the main thread and transferred, not cloned; the mask-drawing loop stamps along pointer segments and stays trivially short.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible canvas drawing surface role application keyboard fallback, range slider labels aria-valuetext, canvas role img alt, live status region, toggle aria-pressed
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give informative canvases role=img + a descriptive name; expose an interactive drawing surface with an accessible name and a keyboard-operable alternative path; use aria-pressed for toggle buttons; label every control; use a single polite live region; style :focus-visible.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — result/fill/base canvases have role=img + descriptive aria-labels; the paint overlay is role=application with an aria-label pointing to the Rectangle-preset/Clear buttons, which give a full keyboard path to a valid mask (the freehand brush stays an enhancement, not the only way in); the Brush/Erase toggle uses aria-pressed; the brush range + mask-tool buttons are labelled; a single role=status aria-live=polite reports progress/results; :focus-visible rings throughout.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two column image editor + controls no horizontal overflow flexbox intrinsic sizing logical properties, canvas overlay editor
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> + min-inline-size:0 so wide flex items shrink instead of overflowing; size media/canvases with inline-size:100% block-size:auto (and a max-inline-size cap on the paint stage); use logical properties; auto-fill/minmax grids for galleries.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the image-editor and results columns use flex:1 1 300px with min-inline-size:0 and stack on narrow viewports; the paint stage is max-inline-size:640px with the base + overlay canvases at inline-size:100% block-size:auto (overlay absolutely positioned over the base); the example gallery is a repeat(auto-fill,minmax()) grid. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800 on the index and all four use-case pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move long ONNX inference and dense per-pixel post-processing off main thread web worker transfer ImageBitmap, canvas mask drawing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (inference + dense pixel loops) into a worker; transfer buffers/ImageBitmaps instead of cloning; keep pointer-driven drawing loops short.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the ~13 s ONNX inpainting pass, the RGBA clamp, the mask composite, the fill map, and the display-aspect resize all run in a module worker; results are transferred back as ImageBitmaps (zero-copy). The main thread only draws bitmaps and stamps brush circles along pointer segments (a trivially short loop), so INP stays low even during a long inpaint.

openQuestions: LaMa is run at a fixed 512×512; a follow-up could tile high-resolution images with overlap and per-tile masks so large photos are inpainted at full detail rather than downscaled to 512.

The 208 MB fp32 download is heavy for mobile data — worth investigating a validated int8/quantised LaMa export (verified non-degenerate) to cut the transfer, if one exists, or a WebGPU ORT-web EP path to cut the ~13 s WASM latency, once verifiable on a GPU runner.

followUpGoals.goal: Add an auto-mask wild demo: run an on-device segmentation/detection model (e.g. SlimSAM / a detector) to propose the removal mask, then LaMa fills it — one-click object removal without manual brushing.
Add a scratch/watermark-removal practical demo tuned for document restoration (thin-mask brush over dust/date-stamps) alongside the object-removal one.

summary: New demo: on-device IMAGE INPAINTING via LaMa (Carve/LaMa-ONNX, big-lama, Apache-2.0, ~208 MB fp32) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a module worker, with the dense output composite (clamp + orig·(1−mask)+lama·mask blend + fill map + resize) off the main thread and transferred as ImageBitmaps. Runnability was proven first in headless Chrome (image+mask [1,3,512,512]/[1,1,512,512] → [0,255] output; a masked hole filled with real structure, mask convention 1=hole confirmed); the index + 4 ladder pages (basics brush-then-fill / practical privacy-redaction+download / wild growing-hole breaking-point / multi-model inpaint→super-res) were build-verified with real download + inference (12.8 s WASM, coherent alley-archway fill reviewed), a Pointer-Events mask editor (brush/erase/rectangle/clear, touch-action:none, with a keyboard-operable rectangle+clear fallback), cached auto-init, an honest empty-mask error, clean console, and 0 px overflow on mobile+desktop across all five pages. Sample images come from the rights-safe media library; untouched pixels are composited through pixel-exact, and the copy is honest that LaMa reconstructs plausible content, not the truth.
